description: SAP (Gateway OData, Fiori, x-csrf-token Fetch handshake) priors.
llmFlowNotes: SAP Gateway OData: the CSRF token is fetched with a GET carrying header 'X-CSRF-Token: Fetch'; the server returns it in the X-CSRF-Token RESPONSE header. Extract that response header and send it as the X-CSRF-Token request header on every following POST/PUT/DELETE. Replaying a recorded token gives 403 'CSRF token validation failed'.
SAP: session is carried by MYSAPSSO2 / SAP_SESSIONID_* cookies — let the HTTP Cookie Manager own them; do not correlate cookies manually.
SAP: sap-client and sap-language are static config — keep them as literals, do not parameterize.
expectations: X-CSRF-Token fetched from response header and reused on mutating calls
SAP session cookies owned by the Cookie Manager
